originalTitle: At least 100 deaths reported in Ebola outbreak in DR Congo as six Americans exposed
title: Heroes Helping: Staying Safe and Healthy in DR Congo!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! Your friendly African news reporter is here to share some important news about staying healthy and strong. Sometimes, grown-ups talk about tiny, tiny germs that can make people feel unwell. Today, we're talking about something called Ebola. It's a special kind of germ that can make people very, very sick. But don't worry, we have lots of heroes helping, and we know how to stay safe!

What is Ebola?

Ebola is like a very strong cold germ. It's so small you cannot see it without a special magnifying glass. When this germ gets inside a person, it can make them have a fever, feel tired, have a tummy ache, and just not feel well at all. It's a serious sickness. But here is a very important thing to remember: Ebola does NOT spread easily! It doesn't fly through the air like a normal sneeze. You only get it if you touch the body fluids of someone who is VERY, VERY sick with Ebola. Things like their sweat or blood. That's why doctors and nurses are super careful. Did you know? The name 'Ebola' comes from a river in Africa! A long time ago, people found this sickness near that river.

Where is this Happening?

This sickness is mostly in one part of a beautiful country called the Democratic Republic of Congo. Congo is a huge country in Africa, with amazing forests, rivers, and very kind people. It's a place where children love to play and learn, just like you! In one area of Congo, some people have gotten sick with Ebola. It's not happening all over Africa, and it's not happening all over Congo. It's in just a few places. Think of it like a small puddle in a very big garden. Sadly, we have heard that some people have become very sick, and some have passed away. And doctors are taking care of many people who have caught this germ. This is very sad news, but it also tells us why the brave doctors and nurses are working so hard.

Who are the Heroes Helping?

Guess what? There are so many heroes working right now to help people in Congo! Doctors, nurses, and health workers are like superheroes without capes. They are wearing special suits, gloves, and masks to keep themselves safe while they care for the sick. They are so brave! These heroes are doing many important things. They are giving medicine to people who are sick, helping them feel better. They are also teaching everyone how to wash their hands properly and how to keep germs away. They work day and night to stop the germ from spreading to more people. Did you know? Health workers in Africa are some of the bravest people in the world! They often travel far to help others and teach about staying healthy.

How Do We Stay Safe?

Even though Ebola is mostly far away, it's always good to know how to stay healthy and safe from ALL kinds of germs, not just Ebola! The best superpower you have against germs is WASHING YOUR HANDS! Use soap and clean water. Sing a happy song while you wash for at least 20 seconds. Wash before you eat, after you play, and after you use the toilet. Clean hands keep you healthy! Another way to stay safe is to avoid touching people who are very sick. If someone has a bad cough or a fever, it's best to give them some space. And if you ever feel unwell, tell a grown-up right away. They will help you.

People from Other Countries are Helping Too!

Because this is a serious sickness, people from all over the world are helping. Doctors and scientists from different countries are working with the doctors in Congo. They are sharing ideas and working together as a big team. Sometimes, if people travel from places where the sickness is, doctors will check them very carefully. This is just to make sure everyone is healthy and that the germ doesn't travel to new places. It's like making sure everyone has a clean bill of health before they go on a big adventure. We heard that a few people who travelled from other countries are being checked. This shows how everyone is being careful to stop the germ. It's all part of making sure everyone stays safe and healthy.

We Are Strong Together!

It's important to remember that most places in Africa are very safe and healthy. This Ebola situation is in a small part of one country, and many amazing people are working hard to make it better. Africa is a huge continent with many, many countries, amazing animals, and wonderful people. This little problem with a germ is something we are learning to fix together. We are brave, we are smart, and we help each other. Did you know? Scientists are working very hard to make special medicines and even vaccines (like a shield for your body) to help fight Ebola! So, my dear friends, remember to wash your hands, listen to your grown-ups, and know that there are many heroes making sure everyone stays safe and healthy in our big, beautiful African home. Keep smiling!

originalTitle: What is Ebola and why is stopping this outbreak so difficult?
title: Hey Super Kids! Let's Learn About Staying Safe in Africa!
content:
Hello, amazing kids of Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here to share some important news. I hope you're all doing great and staying super smart! Today, we're going to talk about something important happening in a part of our big, beautiful continent. It's about a tiny, tiny germ called Ebola. Don't worry, it's not near everyone, but it's good to learn. Brave people are working super hard to stop it from making anyone sick. We call it an 'outbreak' when a lot of people get the same sickness at the same time. This outbreak is happening in a country called DR Congo. Let's learn more together!

What is Ebola?

So, what is Ebola? Imagine a tiny, tiny germ, so small you can't even see it without a special microscope! This germ is called Ebola. It's a type of sickness that can make people feel very, very unwell. It's not like having a little cold or a tummy ache that goes away quickly. Ebola is more serious, and it can make people very tired and feverish. Ebola mostly spreads when people touch someone who is very sick with it. Or if they touch things that a sick person has just touched, like their clothes or blankets. That's why doctors and nurses are very careful. They wear special suits to protect themselves! These suits look a bit like astronaut outfits, but they are for helping people on Earth. Did you know? The name Ebola comes from a river in Africa! A long, long time ago, when doctors first learned about this germ, they named it after a river close to where it was found.

Where is DR Congo?

Now, let's find out where this is all happening. DR Congo is a very big and beautiful country right here in Africa! It's in the middle part of our continent. If you look at a map of Africa, you'll see it right in the heart. It has amazing forests, huge rivers, and many wonderful animals. It's far away from many of your homes, but everyone in Africa is like one big family. So, when one part of our family needs help, we all care. The people there are strong and wonderful, and they need our good thoughts and support. Did you know? DR Congo is home to lots of incredible wildlife, like gorillas and okapis! An okapi looks a bit like a zebra and a giraffe mixed together. Isn't that cool?

Why is this Ebola different?

Sometimes, germs can be a little bit different, even if they have the same name. Think of apples! You have red apples, green apples, and yellow apples, right? They are all apples, but they taste a little bit different. Well, this Ebola germ is a 'rare strain.' That means it's a slightly different kind of Ebola. Scientists are like super-sleuth detectives! They are studying this special kind of Ebola very carefully. They want to understand everything about it so they can help doctors find the best way to make people better. It's a bit tricky because it's not the usual kind they have seen before. But scientists are very smart and they are working super hard every single day to learn all about it. They are finding out how it works and what medicines might help best. This helps them know how to stop it faster.

What is 'Conflict'?

Now, here's a part that makes helping a bit harder. The place where this Ebola outbreak is happening has something we call 'conflict.' Conflict means when people in a place are having big arguments or disagreements, and sometimes they even fight. It's like when friends argue, but much bigger and more serious. It makes everyone feel scared and unsafe. When there is conflict, it's very hard for the helpful doctors and nurses to go to those places. Imagine trying to give medicine to someone when there are loud noises or people are worried. It's tough. The roads might not be safe, or people might be afraid to let doctors come and help them. This conflict means people might not trust each other easily. So, it's harder for health workers to talk to everyone and teach them how to stay safe from Ebola. They might not be able to build hospitals or clinics easily. It's like trying to build a sandcastle on a very windy day – it's just much harder. Did you know? When people stop fighting and work together, amazing things can happen! Peace helps everyone feel safe and makes it easier to solve problems like sickness.

How do we stop Ebola?

Even though it's a tricky germ, we know how to fight Ebola! The best way to stop it is by being super clean. The most important thing is to wash your hands often with soap and water. Scrub, scrub, scrub! Make sure you wash them for a long time, like singing 'Happy Birthday' twice! Doctors and nurses also help by giving special care to sick people. They use clean tools and wear those special suits to protect themselves. This stops the germ from spreading to others. When someone gets sick, they need to rest and get special medicine from the doctors. Sometimes, there are special shots called vaccines. A vaccine is like a shield that helps your body learn to fight off the germ before you even get sick. Scientists are working hard to make sure everyone who needs it can get this shield. It's a big team effort! Did you know? Washing your hands with soap and water can get rid of millions of tiny germs! It's like magic for staying healthy!

Who are the Helpers?

There are so many brave and kind people working to stop Ebola! We have amazing doctors and super nurses who are taking care of the sick. We also have community health workers. These are people from the local area who help teach everyone about staying safe and healthy. Big groups like the World Health Organization (WHO) and UNICEF are also helping. They send doctors, medicines, and supplies to DR Congo. They also help teach people about what to do to stay safe. They are like giant helping teams from all over the world! These helpers are very brave. They travel to difficult places and work tirelessly. They want to make sure everyone is safe and healthy. They are truly African heroes, working for our African family! Did you know? Many different countries and groups work together to help stop outbreaks like Ebola. It's a big global family trying to help!

What can WE do?

Even though you are kids, you can help too! First, you can learn all about staying clean and healthy. Remember to wash your hands often, especially after playing and before eating. Tell your friends and family how important it is too! You can also send your happy thoughts and good wishes to the people in DR Congo. Think about the brave doctors and nurses and wish them strength. Knowing that people care makes a big difference. And the most important thing is to be kind to everyone. When we are kind and help each other, it makes our world a better place. Let's remember that we are all part of the big African family, and we care for each other. Did you know? Sharing your knowledge about hygiene with your family makes everyone safer and healthier! So, even though Ebola is a serious germ, remember that many smart and kind people are working very, very hard to stop it. They are doing everything they can in DR Congo. With all these brave helpers, and with everyone learning to be clean, we can help keep our continent healthy and strong. Thank you for being such amazing listeners, super kids! Stay safe and keep shining bright!
summary: Brave helpers are working hard to stop a tiny germ called Ebola in DR Congo, a country in Africa, but it's tricky because the germ is a rare type and there is fighting in the area.
whyMatters: It's important because we want everyone in our African family to be healthy and safe, and when we understand problems, we can help the people who are trying to solve them.

originalTitle: What is the UAE’s Barakah nuclear plant, nearly hit by a drone?
title: Barakah: The Giant Power Maker Far Away!
content:
Hello, amazing kids! It's your friendly reporter from Africa! Today, we're going on an exciting adventure far, far away. We're going to learn about a super special place in a country called the United Arab Emirates, or UAE for short. It's a country with big, tall buildings and lots of sunshine!

What is Barakah?

Imagine a super big factory. But this factory doesn't make toys or clothes. It makes something very important that we all use every day! This amazing place is called the Barakah Nuclear Energy Plant. It's a very big name for a very big place! It's like a giant, giant kitchen where they cook up something invisible but super powerful. What do they cook? They cook up electricity!

What is Electricity?

"Electricity!" you might say. "What is that?" Well, electricity is like magic! It's the power that makes things work. It's what lights up our homes when it gets dark outside. It's what makes our televisions show cartoons! Electricity helps our phones charge so we can talk to family. It keeps our food cold in the fridge. It even helps doctors in hospitals use special machines to help people feel better. Wow, electricity is super important, isn't it? *Did you know?* Electricity can travel very fast, almost like a superhero! It zooms through wires to get to our homes and schools.

How Does Barakah Make Electricity?

Now, how does this Barakah plant make all this amazing electricity? It uses something very special. It uses tiny, tiny parts of a kind of rock. These tiny parts can make a lot of heat when they are put together in a very safe way. Imagine heating a giant pot of water. When water gets super hot, it turns into steam, right? This steam is very powerful. The Barakah plant uses this powerful steam to spin giant machines called turbines. Think of a turbine like a big spinning wheel, like a pinwheel but much, much bigger! When the steam pushes these big wheels, they spin super fast. And when these big wheels spin, they make electricity! It's a bit like riding a bicycle that lights up a small bulb – but on a super-duper large scale! *Did you know?* The energy that comes from these special rocks is called "nuclear energy." It's a very strong kind of energy!

How Much Power Does Barakah Make?

The Barakah plant is not just big; it's super powerful! It can make enough electricity to power a lot of homes. The people in the UAE need a lot of electricity for their big cities and homes. This one plant can make enough electricity for "one-quarter" of the whole country's needs. What does "one-quarter" mean? Imagine a yummy pizza cut into four equal slices. If Barakah makes one slice of electricity for the whole country, that's a lot! So, if the UAE needs four slices of electricity every day, the Barakah plant makes one whole big slice all by itself! That means it helps keep lights on in so many places, from big schools to tall office buildings. It's a huge help for the country. *Did you know?* Having enough electricity helps a country grow and makes life better for everyone!

Why is Barakah Important for the UAE?

Making so much electricity from one place is very helpful for the UAE. It means they don't have to burn as much oil or gas to make electricity. Burning oil and gas can sometimes make the air not as clean. Using nuclear energy from Barakah helps them have clean air. It's a way to make power that is good for our Earth. It helps them have enough power for all their needs, for today and for many, many years to come. It’s a very smart way to make energy! It also means the UAE can be sure they will always have enough power. They won't run out of electricity easily. This helps all the people living and working there. *Did you know?* Thinking about how we make electricity helps us take care of our planet!

Safety First at Barakah

Making so much power needs super clever people and super safe rules. The Barakah plant has very, very strict rules for safety. Lots of smart engineers and scientists work there every day to make sure everything is working perfectly and safely. They check everything all the time, just like your teachers check if you are safe at school. They make sure the special rocks are handled with great care. Their number one job is to keep everyone safe and keep the electricity flowing smoothly.

Learning from Far Away

Even though the Barakah plant is in a country far away from Africa, we can still learn from it! Countries all over the world, including our own countries here in Africa, need electricity. We need power for our homes, our schools, and our hospitals. Learning about how other countries make power, even with new and different ways like nuclear energy, helps us think about how we can make our own countries stronger and brighter. Maybe one day, we will have our own super power makers too! *Did you know?* Scientists are always looking for new and better ways to make electricity!

A Bright Future with Power

So, the Barakah plant is a giant, clever factory in the UAE that makes tons of electricity. It lights up homes, powers schools, and helps a whole country grow. It shows us how big ideas can make a really big difference for everyone. Remember, electricity is super important for our world. And learning about places like Barakah helps us understand how amazing our world is and how smart people work to make it better for all of us. That's all for today from your friendly African news reporter! Stay curious and keep learning, my amazing little friends!
summary: The Barakah plant in the UAE is a very big factory that uses special rocks to make a lot of electricity, enough to power one-quarter of the country's homes and schools safely and cleanly.
whyMatters: This is important because electricity helps our homes, schools, and hospitals work, and learning how a big plant like Barakah makes power helps us think about making our own countries brighter and stronger with enough energy for everyone.

originalTitle: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt booed by graduates at mention of AI
title: When Smart Computers Made Grown-Ups Go 'Boo'!
content:
Jambo, amazing friends! It's your Kid Reporter, Jojo, here from sunny Africa, ready with a super cool story for you! Today, we're going to talk about super-smart computers, big students, and a little bit of a surprise! Grab your listening ears, because this is going to be fun.

Meet a Very Smart Grown-Up

Imagine a giant company that helps you find almost anything on the internet! Like how to draw a lion, or videos of baby elephants, or even how to make a yummy cake. That company is called Google! A very important grown-up named Mr. Eric Schmidt used to be in charge of Google. He is super, super smart and knows a lot about computers and technology. He has helped Google become one of the biggest and most helpful companies in the world! So, when he talks, people usually listen very carefully.

What Happened at the Big School Party?

Mr. Eric Schmidt was visiting a big school where students had just finished their many years of learning. These students are called 'graduates'. They were celebrating finishing school and getting ready for their 'grown-up' lives. Mr. Schmidt was talking to them about new and exciting things in the world of computers. He started to talk about something very clever called 'AI'. Now, guess what? When he said the word 'AI', some of the students started to make 'boo' noises! Not everyone, but enough to make people notice. It was a little bit surprising, right?

What is AI? (It's Not a Robot Alien!)

AI sounds like a secret code, but it just stands for **A**rtificial **I**ntelligence. Artificial means it's made by people, not born like you or me. And intelligence means it's super smart! So, AI is like giving a computer a super-smart brain! This smart computer brain can learn things, solve puzzles, and even do tasks. It's not a real person or a talking robot alien, but it can do many amazing things. **Did you know?** AI helps your parent's phone take really good pictures! It knows how to make the colours look beautiful. AI can help computers understand what you say when you ask a question. It can even help draw pictures or write stories! It's like having a super helper that lives inside a computer. **Did you know?** Some cars use AI to help the driver park safely! It's like having an extra pair of smart eyes.

Why Were the Students Worried?

The students who made 'boo' noises were not being mean. They were feeling a little bit worried inside. They had just finished school and were ready to find 'jobs'. A job is when grown-ups do something helpful, like teaching, building, farming, or telling stories, and they get money for it. This money helps them buy food, clothes, and a home. The students were worried because they thought that maybe, just maybe, these super-smart AI computers might start doing some of the jobs that people usually do. **Did you know?** In Africa, many people work as farmers, teachers, and doctors! These jobs are very important. They wondered, "If computers can do so much, will there be enough jobs for us when we grow up?" That's a big question, and it made them feel a little scared about their future.

AI Can Be a Super Friend!

It's true that AI is very clever, but it's important to remember that AI is a **tool**. Think of a hammer. A hammer is a tool that helps a builder build a house. The hammer doesn't build the house all by itself, does it? The builder uses the hammer. AI is like that! It can help people do their jobs even better and faster. For example, AI can help doctors find out what is making someone sick much quicker. This means doctors can help more people get well! **Did you know?** AI can help scientists learn about faraway planets and even discover new medicines! AI can also help farmers know the best time to plant and water their crops, so we can all have more yummy food to eat! It makes things more efficient and helps us solve big problems. **Did you know?** Some apps you play on your tablet or phone use AI to make the game fun and challenging!

New Jobs for the Future!

It's possible that some jobs might change because of AI. But new jobs will also be created! Think about it: when cars were invented, people didn't need horse-and-carriage drivers anymore. But then we needed car mechanics, road builders, and taxi drivers! New technology often brings new kinds of jobs that we can't even imagine yet. Maybe in the future, there will be jobs like 'AI trainer' (teaching the smart computers what to do!), or 'robot fixer', or even 'AI story creator'! **Did you know?** When the internet first started, nobody knew that one day people would have jobs as 'YouTubers' or 'App Developers'! So, instead of being scared, we can be excited about learning new things and finding new ways to work with these clever computer helpers.

What Can YOU Do?

So, what does this mean for YOU, my smart little friends? It means that the world is always changing, and that's okay! The best thing you can do is keep learning every single day. Learn to read, learn to count, learn about animals, learn about computers! Be curious! Ask questions! Try new things! The more you learn, the smarter your brain gets, and the more ready you will be for any new changes or new jobs that come along. **Did you know?** Your brain is like a muscle! The more you use it to learn, the stronger and smarter it becomes! In Africa, many young people are learning about technology and AI right now. They are creating amazing new ideas to help their communities and make our continent even better!

Let's Be Brave and Smart!

So, the next time you hear about AI, remember that it's a super-smart tool. It's like a special pencil or a powerful tractor. It helps people do wonderful things. We just need to learn how to use it in the best way! It's okay for grown-ups to feel a little worried sometimes, but it's also important to be brave and keep learning. Your future is going to be full of amazing adventures and new discoveries. Keep smiling, keep learning, and your future will be super bright and full of wonderful jobs that might not even exist yet! That's all for today from your friend, Jojo! Bye for now!
summary: Some grown-up students made 'boo' noises when a very smart man talked about 'AI' (smart computers) because they were worried AI might take their future jobs, but AI can also be a helpful tool that creates new kinds of work.
whyMatters: This story is important because it shows us that new things, like super-smart computers, can sometimes make grown-ups a little worried about their future jobs, but it also teaches us that learning new things helps us be ready for anything, and smart computers can help us in many good ways too.

content: Mohamed Salah should be dropped from Liverpool's season finale for "selfish" criticism of the club, says Wayne Rooney.

originalTitle: West Ham relegation may cost London taxpayers £2.5m
title: Big News About Football and Money from Far Away!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! Your kid-friendly news reporter from Africa is here! I have a very interesting story to share with you today. It’s about football, big stadiums, and lots of money. This story comes from a big city far, far away called London. Let's learn all about it!

Meet West Ham United!

First, let's talk about a football team. There is a team called West Ham United. They are a famous football team in London. Do you love watching football? Football is a game where two teams try to kick a ball into the other team's goal. It's super exciting! West Ham United has many fans. Fans are people who love the team and cheer for them. They wear the team's colours and shout loudly when West Ham scores a goal. Football is one of the most popular games in the whole world! Did you know? In some places, football is called soccer! It's the same game, just a different name.

The Big London Stadium

Every big football team needs a place to play. West Ham United plays in a very, very big place called London Stadium. It is a giant building made just for sports games. Imagine a really big hall where thousands and thousands of people can sit and watch. This stadium is not just any stadium. It is a very special place. Many big events happen there, not just football. It is like a super-sized playground for grown-ups and their games. It's amazing to see! Did you know? Some stadiums are so big that they can hold more people than live in many small towns! They are truly gigantic!

Playing Up and Playing Down: What is Relegation?

Now, let's talk about something called 'relegation'. This is a bit tricky, but I will make it simple for you. Imagine there are different 'classes' for football teams, just like you have Class 1, Class 2, and Class 3 at school. West Ham United plays in the very best 'class' of football teams in England. This best class is called the Premier League. It's where all the strongest teams play against each other. But what happens if a team doesn't play so well? If a team loses too many games and ends up at the bottom of the 'class', they might have to go to a lower 'class' next year. This is called 'relegation'. It means they go down to a less important league. It's like if you are in Class 2, but you don't do well, you might have to stay in Class 1 again. Teams try very hard to avoid relegation because it's not good for them. They want to stay in the top league! Did you know? When a team gets relegated, it usually means they earn less money too! That's why they try so hard to win.

Grown-Ups and Their Money: What are Taxpayers?

Next, let's learn about grown-ups and their money. When grown-ups work, they earn money. This money helps them buy food, clothes, and homes. But they also give some of their money to the government. This special money is called 'tax'. Why do they give their money to the government? Because the government uses this money to help everyone. They build schools for children like you. They build hospitals to help sick people. They build roads so cars can drive safely. These grown-ups who pay tax are called 'taxpayers'. So, taxpayers are like big helpers for the whole city or country. Their money makes many good things happen for everyone. It's a very important job! Did you know? Every time your parents buy something in a shop, a small part of that money often goes to the government as tax!

Renting a Big House: The Lease Agreement

Remember the big London Stadium where West Ham plays? Well, West Ham United does not own that stadium. They 'rent' it. Renting is like borrowing something big for a long time and paying money for it every month or year. It's like if your family rents a house to live in. When you rent something, you have a special paper. This paper is called a 'lease agreement'. It's like a big promise or a set of rules. It says how much money West Ham has to pay to use the stadium. It also says what happens if things change. This lease agreement is very important. It has all the rules written down. It is a very serious paper for grown-ups. It helps everyone know what to do. Did you know? Most big companies and teams rent their buildings. It's very common!

The Big Problem: Money, Money, Money!

Now, here is the big news! The grown-ups in London might have to pay extra money if West Ham United gets relegated. Oh no! This is because of that special 'lease agreement' we just talked about. If West Ham goes down to a lower league, they might not earn as much money. Because of the rules in their agreement, if they earn less, the city of London (which owns the stadium) might have to cover some of the costs. This means the London taxpayers – those grown-ups paying tax – might have to pay an extra £2.5 million! Wow, £2.5 million! That is a super huge amount of money. Imagine two million and five hundred thousand tiny little coins. It's so much money that it's hard to even imagine! It is a lot of money that could be used for other things. This extra money would come from the taxpayers' pockets. That means money that could have been used for new schools or better roads might have to go to the stadium instead. This is why it's a big deal for the city and its people.

What Could £2.5 Million Buy?

Let's think about what £2.5 million could buy. It could buy hundreds of thousands of new books for school libraries. It could help build many, many new classrooms for children. It could buy lots of food to feed many families for a very long time. Imagine how many footballs you could buy with that money! A single good football might cost about £20. So, £2.5 million could buy about 125,000 footballs! That's enough for every child in a huge city to have their own ball to play with. This money is a big deal because it can do so much good. That's why everyone hopes it won't have to be paid. It just shows how important even sports games can be for a city! Did you know? If you saved £1 every day, it would take you more than 6,800 years to save £2.5 million! That's a very, very long time!

Hoping for the Best!

So, what happens next? Everyone is hoping that West Ham United plays very, very well in their football games. If they play their best and win enough games, they will stay in the top Premier League. If they stay up, then the grown-ups in London won't have to pay that extra £2.5 million. It's a big reminder that doing your best is important, not just for you, but sometimes for many other people too! Let's all cheer for West Ham United from far away. We hope they play super well! This story shows us how even a football game can be connected to big money decisions for a whole city. Keep playing, keep learning, and keep doing your best, just like the football teams! Bye for now!
summary: Grown-ups in London might have to pay £2.5 million extra if their local football team, West Ham United, moves to a lower league because of a special agreement about their stadium.
whyMatters: This matters because if West Ham United doesn't play well and gets moved to a lower league, a lot of money that grown-ups pay as tax will have to be used for the stadium instead of for things like new schools or better roads for everyone.

originalTitle: Giroud, Azpilicueta and McCarthy join BBC World Cup team
title: Giroud, Azpilicueta and McCarthy join BBC World Cup team
content: BBC Sport announces its plans for the 2026 Fifa World Cup next month including coverage of all 104 matches in the United States, Canada and Mexico.
